features.summary: UnitedHealth Group reported strong Q1 2022 results with revenue growing 14% to $80 billion, driven by double-digit growth at both Optum and UnitedHealthcare. The company raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance by $0.10 to $21.20-$21.70. OptumHealth showed particularly strong momentum with 34% revenue growth and over 40% earnings growth, adding 600,000 patients under value-based arrangements (up from initial estimate of 500,000). UnitedHealthcare remains on track to add 800,000 Medicare Advantage members in 2022, with strong growth in dual special needs plans.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in their long-term 13% to 16% EPS growth strategy, highlighting five key growth areas: value-based care delivery, health benefits innovation, health technology partnerships, health financial services, and pharmacy services. They expect OptumHealth to continue robust growth with expanding home and community care capabilities, particularly following the pending LHC Group acquisition. The company sees significant runway for continued value-based care expansion and expects strong performance from new health system partnerships and innovative benefit offerings like virtual-first plans.
features.risks: The company faces inflationary pressures across labor and other inputs, requiring targeted investments in clinicians and customer service staff where higher attrition is observed. Care utilization patterns remain somewhat volatile, with emergency department and pediatric visits still below baseline levels. Management continues monitoring for potential acuity increases from deferred care during the pandemic, though no significant signals have emerged yet. The pending Change Healthcare acquisition faces regulatory challenges, though the company remains committed to the combination.
features.key_drivers: Value-based care expansion is the primary growth driver, with OptumHealth adding patients at an accelerated pace and achieving strong clinical outcomes like 25% reduction in skilled nursing facility admits in new markets. Medicare Advantage growth remains strong with 5-star quality ratings enabling year-round enrollment. Innovation in virtual care and home-based services is driving member adoption, with 90% of new individual exchange enrollees selecting plans with virtual components. Pharmacy services are showing strong retention and new business wins, with specialty cost management solutions reducing costs by over 15%.
